chief_complaint: A 58-year-old hypertensive man presented with severe central chest pain and palpitation.
history: The patient is a 58-year-old hypertensive man with no other mentioned past medical history, medications, or social history.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are mentioned.
labs: ECG showed fast atrial fibrillation with features suggestive of left main coronary artery occlusion. Labs also showed high D-dimer and lactate. Coronary angiogram (CAG) showed no stenosis. Bedside Transthoracic echocardiography (TTE) showed a dissecting flap in the ascending aorta. CT revealed Stanford Type-A Aortic Dissection (AD).
